name: Elyndor: The Age of Shadows
description:
Before the Black Witch. Before the Fall. Before Luminor.

A WORLD ON THE EDGE OF RUIN

Three thousand years before the Kingdom of Luminor united a shattered continent, the world of Elyndor teetered on the brink of collapse.

This is the age where strength is truth and morality is a luxury for the naive. Where human ambition claws toward godhood through forbidden hunts. Where elven wisdom retreats into ancient forests, preparing to sever ties with a world gone mad. Where demi-human tribes roar defiance from mountain peaks, refusing to bow to those who would enslave them.

And at the center of it all—a prodigy who should never have existed.

Nyxia Eclipsara. Born human, disowned for her “unnatural” affinity for shadows. Adopted by the Phoenix Emperor himself. The first and only mortal to achieve Magus level—a power that transcends the boundaries between mortal and divine.

She stands at the precipice between hope and catastrophe. Her brilliance inspires students of all races. Her power terrifies kings who see their supremacy threatened. Her existence proves that the old hierarchies can be shattered.

But envy is a poison that seeps through cracks. And when the powerful feel threatened, they do not bow—they hunt.

This is the story of how a world’s greatest light became its darkest shadow.

This is the age before the Black Witch’s fall.

THE POWER SYSTEM: MANA CIRCULATION

In Elyndor, mana circulation techniques are everything. They determine your magical ability, social status, and right to exist. Traditional weapons are scorned as tools of the weak—true power flows from within.

The Grade Hierarchy

Grade 8-7 — Primitive Flows

Lowborn humans, common workers, tribal basics. Crude elemental bursts, basic body enhancement. Unstable and exhausting. “The foundation most never escape.”

Grade 6-5 — Refined Flows

Mid-tier nobles, elite warriors, grove keepers. Enhanced elemental control, minor illusions, summoning. Stable but limited. “Respectability, but not true power.”

Grade 4-3 — Advanced Flows

High nobles, royal heirs, tribal warlords. Hybrid elements, soul manipulation, environmental control. Generational secrets requiring bloodline purity or forbidden rituals. “The realm of kings and legends.”

Grade 2 — Sovereign Flows

Legendary rulers, ancient prodigies. Celestial forces, forbidden hybrids. Rare and often unstable. “Where mortals touch divinity—and risk madness.”

Grade 1 — Divine Flows

Divine beasts only. Reality-rewriting power. Eternal flames that resurrect. Tidal forces that reshape coastlines. Earth-shattering roars. “The domain of gods.”

Mastery Levels

Regardless of grade, practitioners advance through universal stages:

Novice → Expert → Master → Grandmaster → Magus

Each breakthrough requires talent, resources, and risk. Failure can cripple or kill. Reaching Magus means transcending your technique’s limits—rewriting its very foundations.

Only one mortal in history has achieved it: Nyxia Eclipsara.

THE FOUR KINGDOMS
